ekern_transcription.label: Full ekern transcription
ekern_transcription.expectation: Expected to fail / hallucinate — full symbolic transcription requires fine-tuning. Included as a lower-bound probe.
ekern_transcription.prompt:
Transcribe this piano sheet music image into ekern notation (**ekern_1.0). Ekern is a two-column tab-separated format: left column = bass clef staff, right column = treble clef staff. Each row is a beat position. Use standard kern tokens: note durations (1, 2, 4, 8, 16), pitch (C, D, E, F, G, A, B with octave suffix), rests (r), barlines (=), clef (*clefG2, *clefF4), key (*k[b-]), time (*M4/4), voice beaming (L=beam start, J=beam end). Example first line: **ekern_1.0	**ekern_1.0
Transcribe as many measures as you can read.
ekern_transcription.response:
I can’t reliably read individual pitches/chords from this full-page scan (the noteheads/accidentals and many stacked chords are not legible enough at this zoom), so I can’t produce an accurate **kern transcription without guessing. If you can upload a higher-resolution scan (or cropped close-ups of a few measures—e.g., the first 4 measures of INTRO and the first 8 measures of WALTZ), I can transcribe them precisely.

What I *can* provide from what’s clearly visible is the basic **kern scaffold (two staves, waltz feel in 3/4) laid out as “one row per beat” with barlines, but with notes left as rests to avoid inventing pitches:

```text
**ekern_1.0	**ekern_1.0
*clefF4	*clefG2
*M3/4	*M3/4
*k[]	*k[]
=1	=1
4r	4r
4r	4r
4r	4r
=2	=2
4r	4r
4r	4r
4r	4r
=3	=3
4r	4r
4r	4r
4r	4r
=4	=4
4r	4r
4r	4r
4r	4r
*-	*-
```

If you want, tell me **which section** to start with (“INTRO” or “WALTZ”) and upload a **tight crop** of just that system at higher resolution (so staff lines and accidentals are crisp). Then I’ll transcribe as many measures as are legible into real pitch/chord tokens.
